title: Quick check: Enhanced Conversions and Consent Mode

questions.prompt: What does Enhanced Conversions actually do?

choices.text: Sends hashed (SHA-256) first-party user data — email, phone, name, address — alongside the conversion event. Google matches the hashed data to logged-in Google users, recovering attribution that was lost to iOS ATT, Safari ITP, ad blockers, and cookie restrictions. Lift is typically 10-30% more attributed conversions plus improved match quality for Smart Bidding's signal. Email alone helps significantly; adding phone, name, and address improves further. The cumulative effect makes Smart Bidding's per-auction predictions sharper.

questions.explanation: Hashed user data sent with conversions recovers privacy-lost attribution.

questions.prompt: B2B lead-gen account optimizing toward leads, not closed deals. Cause and fix?

choices.text: Missing offline conversion imports. Smart Bidding optimizes toward whatever you tell it counts — if 'Lead' is the primary conversion, it finds users who submit leads, not users who become customers. Fix: capture GCLID on the landing page (hidden form field), pass it to your CRM with the lead, and when the lead closes weeks later, import the closed-deal conversion back to Google with the GCLID as the key (and a meaningful value). For 2026 stack, also implement Enhanced Conversions for Leads so email/phone can bridge attribution when GCLID isn't reliably captured. The system then optimizes for closed deals, not just form submissions.

questions.explanation: Import offline closed-deal conversions with GCLID. Optimize for revenue, not leads.
